summary: Lead accepts M6-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 after systems_architect REVIEW and independent qa_reviewer ACCEPT. docs/GATE-M6.md records M6_GATE = PASS_WITH_LIMITS with Alpha/local limits, preserves DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE, records no accepted P0/P1 blocker, and does not authorize M7 content lock, telemetry, server/multiplayer, arbitrary-code mods, release/commercial/branding decisions, or frozen architecture changes. The branch is accepted pending PR CI, main integration, and closure.

tests_run.result: M6-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 listed as ACCEPTED; all M6 dependencies listed as CLOSED.
No READY output while M6 exit gate is accepted but not yet integrated.
Committed target diff includes only allowed M6 gate, status, task, registry, goal/model-routing, and project/messages paths.
Committed and unstaged diff paths are all within M6-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 allowed_paths.

decisions: ACCEPT M6_GATE = PASS_WITH_LIMITS based on systems_architect review and independent qa_reviewer acceptance.
Keep current_milestone at M6 until PR CI passes, branch merges, main is synced, and the task is CLOSED.
Keep human_gate.required false; no unresolved Human Gate blocks M6 exit integration.
Preserve DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE exactly; this gate creates no manual node battle implementation, spike, or blocking research task.
Do not start M7 content lock from the gate branch; after closure on main, follow taskctl and repository task graph state.
risks: M6 remains PASS_WITH_LIMITS, not plain PASS, because scenario data is Alpha validation scaffolding, adviser evidence is local/privacy-safe, batch balance contains non-blocking tuning risks, prior M4/M5 limits remain historical context, and desktop packaging is smoke evidence rather than a signed release artifact.
requested_action: Push branch, open PR, wait for CI, merge to main, synchronize main, then close M6-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 before selecting the next task.
